description: Backfills the 11 legacy teacher_kpi_config items with the cross-link disciplines established for the 4 extension items in teacher-kpi-extensions-v1.json. Each legacy item gets the missing fields: competency_link (slug into competency_framework/teachers), appraisal_link (F-items in appraisal-framework-v2.json), and weekly_checklist_link (task id from subject-teacher.json). cambridge_standard_refs[] already populated per the prior tag-kpi-cambridge-refs.js run; this backfill does NOT touch those.
rationale: INDEX.md health snapshot showed KPI cross-link integrity at 'Partial' — 4 new items fully linked, 11 legacy items lacked the same link chain. This backfill closes that gap so all 15 teacher KPI items have the same depth of cross-reference. Once applied, every KPI item answers: which Cambridge standard? which competency level? which appraisal evidence? which weekly task produces this evidence? Pedagogically defensible answer for each.
merge_policy: Idempotent. Each item update sets ONLY the four cross-link fields (competency_link, appraisal_link[], weekly_checklist_link, first_time_guide). Existing fields (aspect, indicator, weight, target, rubric, cambridge_standard_refs[]) are NOT touched. { merge: true } at field level. Re-running is safe — admin edits via /kpi-admin to other fields are preserved.

items.competency_link_rationale: afl-2 is the assessment-feedback competency — teachers learn to give feedback connected to Cambridge progression. Sustained student academic improvement is the downstream evidence of effective afl-2 practice.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F2 captures classroom observation evidence of effective instruction driving improvement; F3 captures work inspection evidence (marked student work shows progress).
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: TT-C3-001 'Mark and return one piece of substantive student work this week' is the operational rhythm that produces the evidence of student progress this KPI measures.

items.competency_link_rationale: icp-1 is the active-learning competency. Engagement at the rate this KPI requires (90%) is the visible indicator of icp-1 practice maturity.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F2 (Class Observation) is where engagement is directly observed; the appraiser scores engagement live during the announced lesson.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: TT-C3-002 'Conduct at least 2 formative checks for understanding per lesson' is the weekly discipline that produces the engagement this KPI measures.

items.competency_link_rationale: cce-2 is the parent-communication competency. Sustained student attendance depends on the teacher's communication with parents about absences and engagement.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F1 (Teacher Administration) is where attendance discipline is reviewed — the daily attendance recording habit is administrative diligence.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: TT-C5-001 'Complete attendance and grade entries on time' is the daily compliance rhythm that produces this KPI's evidence.

items.competency_link_rationale: afl-2 covers assessment-for-learning + feedback connected to Cambridge progression. Bi-monthly individual progress assessments + feedback to 100% of students is the operational manifestation of afl-2 mastery.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F3 (Teacher's Work Inspection) is where the appraiser examines student work books for evidence of regular progress feedback.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: TT-C3-001 weekly mark-and-return rhythm aggregates into the bi-monthly individual progress evidence this KPI requires.

items.competency_link_rationale: pie-1 (reflective practice) underpins effective classroom management — the teacher who reflects on what worked and what didn't iteratively improves their routines.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F2 captures classroom climate, transition discipline, and management effectiveness directly during observation.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: TT-W01-001 'Establish 3 non-negotiable classroom routines before any instruction begins' is the foundational task that determines every subsequent classroom management observation.

items.competency_link_rationale: lcp-1 IS the differentiation competency. Implementation of differentiated instruction in 90% of lessons is the direct evidence of lcp-1 practice maturity.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F2 captures live differentiated practice in observation; F3 captures evidence of differentiated tasks in lesson plans + student work.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: TT-C1-001 'Submit lesson plans for the coming week' includes the LPQR Differentiation standard. Plans must explicitly show differentiation provisions — that is where this KPI's evidence is generated.

items.competency_link_rationale: pie-1 (reflective practice + professional learning) is the competency that makes PD attendance meaningful. Without pie-1 maturity, PD becomes a tickbox; with pie-1 maturity, attendance translates into practice change.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F1 (Teacher Administration) reviews IPDL completeness, PD attendance records, and engagement quality.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: TT-C4-001 'Update your Individual Professional Development Log (IPDL)' is the weekly discipline that aggregates into this KPI's annual evidence.

items.competency_link_rationale: pie-2 covers professional standards + ethics, which underpin consistent, fair behaviour management. Maintaining low disciplinary incident rates depends on professional response patterns.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F2 captures behaviour management directly in classroom observation — how the teacher handles low-level disruption + response patterns.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: TT-C5-001 (compliance + administration) covers incident logging, parent communication on incidents, and consistent response rhythm. Complementary to TT-W01-001 (foundational routines).

items.competency_link_rationale: lcp-1 (differentiation for diverse learners) extends to SEN/IEP support. Implementing accommodations for 100% of identified students is the SEN-tier expression of lcp-1 maturity.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F3 (Work Inspection) reviews IEP documentation, modified tasks, and accommodation evidence in student work.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: TT-C1-001 lesson plans must explicitly show SEN modifications referencing each student's ASP/IEP — the LPQR Differentiation standard requires this. That is where the SEN-support evidence is generated weekly.

items.competency_link_rationale: icp-1 (active engagement) is the competency most directly correlated with student satisfaction — students rate teachers higher when engagement is genuine.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F4 IS the Student Survey framework. This KPI is operationally identical with F4's data source.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: Student satisfaction is collected via termly survey, not produced by a specific weekly task. The link is at the F4 framework level, not at the weekly-task level.

items.competency_link_rationale: pie-3 covers safeguarding + child protection AND extends into student wellbeing as a teaching responsibility. SEL activities are the proactive expression of pie-3 maturity.
items.appraisal_link_rationale: F1 reviews evidence of SEL activity planning + execution + reflection. Evidence is administrative rather than observational.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: SEL activities are planned termly (not weekly). The link is at the termly-task level (TT-C4-003 termly self-assessment touches on this) but not at a single weekly task.

items._note: This is the extension item #15 (already complete except for weekly_checklist_link). Setting weekly_checklist_link = null with rationale.
items.weekly_checklist_link_rationale: CLIL compliance is a cross-cutting standard rather than a discrete weekly task. It is enforced via TT-C1-001 (lesson plan must include bilingual support) and TT-C2-002 (walkthrough records language compliance), but neither task is the SOLE source of CLIL evidence — it permeates many tasks. The link sits at the F2 framework level, not at a single weekly-task level.

post_backfill_state._note: Three items legitimately have weekly_checklist_link=null (tk_student_satisfaction = survey-driven; tk_student_wellbeing = termly-driven; tk_bilingual_clil_compliance = cross-cutting). Plus tk_academic_performance variant. These are not gaps — they are pedagogically defensible nulls with rationale.

appraisal_distribution.F2: tk_academic_performance, tk_classroom_engagement, tk_classroom_management, tk_inclusive_teaching, tk_behavior_management, tk_bilingual_clil_compliance

provenance.regulatory_sources: Inherited from teacher_kpi_config base — Permendikbud 6/2018, PP 19/2005 jo. 13/2015
provenance.framework_sources: Cambridge Teacher Standards 2023 (cambridge_standard_refs already present from prior tag-kpi-cambridge-refs.js run), Eduversal Teacher Appraisal Framework v2.1, Eduversal Teacher Competency Framework
provenance.source_integrity_check: All 12 competency_link slugs verified against Central Hub/resources/teaching-competency-framework.json. All 15 weekly_checklist_link references verified against docs/weekly-checklists/subject-teacher.json (or null with rationale).
provenance.audit_trigger: INDEX.md health snapshot 2026-05-08 — KPI integrity at Partial; this backfill moves to Full.
